'''Sam Rapira''' (born 8 April 1987) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] representative he previously played in the [[National Rugby League]] for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. Rapira was also part of the New Zealand team that won the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]].

==Background==
Rapira was in [[Hamilton, New Zealand|Hamilton]], New Zealand.

==Early years==
The older brother of [[Steve Rapira|Steve]], Sam Rapira attended [[Hamilton Boys' High School]] and played for the [[Hukanui]] club.<ref>[http://warriors.sitesuite.ws/plugins/newsfeed.cgi?rm=content&plugin_data_id=25952 World Cup Stars for Vodafone Warriors trial in Hamilton]{{dead link|date=May 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} ''Warriors Official Site''</ref> He later played for the [[Waicoa Bay Stallions]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]]. Rapira first made the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2004, and played for them again in 2005. Also in 2005 he was named in the [[New Zealand Residents]] squad.
[[File:SAM RAPIRA in 2013.jpg|thumb|right|Rapira playing for the Warriors in 2013]]

==Playing career==
===New Zealand Warriors===
Rapira made his NRL debut for the Warriors in 2006 against the [[Wests Tigers]].

In 2007 he played in all 26 games for the club.

In 2011 Rapira extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2014 season.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41722 Rapira commits to Warriors] ''rleague.com'', 13 November 2011</ref> Rapira announced that he was leaving the Warriors at the end of the [[2015 NRL season]] to join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a two season deal from 2016 <ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/rapira-to-join-super-leagues-huddersfield/tabid/10874/newsid/86477/default.aspx |title=Rapira to join Super League's Huddersfield |access-date=2015-07-30 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150626004440/http://www.nrl.com/rapira-to-join-super-leagues-huddersfield/tabid/10874/newsid/86477/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-06-26 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
Rapira spent the 2016 and 2017 seasons playing for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

===Toulouse Olympique===
Rapira joined [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]] ahead of the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41173907|title=Sam Rapira: Huddersfield Giants prop to join Toulouse|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2017-09-06|accessdate=2017-12-13}}</ref>

===International career===
In 2006 he played for New Zealand 'A'.

Rapira was first selected to play for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] from the interchange bench in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test Match 2007|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|accessdate=1 September 2013}}</ref>

In August 2008, Rapira was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]],<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|accessdate=2008-08-08}}</ref> and in October 2008, he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|Kiwi]] squad.<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He was part of the team that won the World Cup Final.

For the [[2010 Anzac Test]], Rapira was selected to play for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at prop forward in their loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].
